title: Postcard of post office in Springfield, Massachusetts (1912)
description: Photograph. Color. Brick building, street corner, pedestrians in foreground
transcription: Transcription Recto: Hope you are well this beautiful Sunday. Transcription Verso: Oct 27 1912. Am at 3 Tanner St. Saw Aunt Sarah + Aunt Ruth both today seems about as usual Aunt Sarah is home Clarence is going to keep house just same without her she grants me to come with Lee I may go later but the people here are nice to me so I shall stay a while any way ask Bertha if this looks natural as about a year ago we visited it together Best wishes to all you (illegible) daughter C(illegible)
creator: blank

title: Postcard of a family at the beach with lobster joke (1910)
description: Illustration. Color. Beachside scene with a boy in a red cap with a red pail (right) speaking to a seated woman in a red dress and floral hat holding reading material (magazine?) (left). Further left of the woman is a man seated on the beach holding a newspaper and wearing a sportscoat and brimmed hat
transcription: Transcription Recto: Hello Elise. I guess I will drop you a postal for I have nothing else to do till 8'o clock because I got a date with my girl you talk about they an't any pretty girls in small towns well you should see this one she has got them all beat in Seattle. Bethel, and all the other towns, I will tell you what she looks like when I get home I also won't be home till Sunday (illegible). Transcription Verso: I am going to a mastergrade dance to-morrow night with my sweetheart, D'you Riddle? How is my Big Brother getting along tell him I am having a swell time we go to work at 7oclock and quit at seven at night (illegible) you see? (illegible) not all in at night time I (illegible) dancing the (illegible) with my girl and we get (illegible) in (illegible) she got you skinned in dancing I think we would of won if I hadn't got nervous at last, The prize was (illegible) and I needd to (illegible) Gee I was sore. Well got (illegible) Brother (illegible)
creator: illegible
subject: United States; Washington; Seattle; Washington; Walla Walla; naturescape

transcription: Transcription Recto: (NA). Transcription Verso: Dear Ned:- How the wind blows, I guess you will blow away going to school. There was an bad accident here yesterday while the children were out at recess the car run over one little boy cut off his arms and " his body in two. Baby is eating his dinner. Write when you can. (illegible) (illegible)

title: Postcard with a photograph of a young woman reclining on a hammock (1910)
description: Photograph. Black-and-white. A young woman with long dark hair wearing a white dress, lying on a hammock. Text reads "There's room for two here."
transcription: Transcription Recto: I don't know if you'll get this, but will send it any way. There's "some class" to those "kodak pictures" you sent, Gee but you're getting to be a "sport." I suppose you'll be glad to see your little wifey (Frank). Don't celebrate to much. Give our love to Frank from all, & keep some for yourself. Transcription Verso: Dear (illegible): Received your letter yesterday, was pleased to get it. I see that you at last are leaving Salt Lake. Ma and I were afraid you'd be a "Mormon" pretty soon, if you didn't leave. You'd better come home before the cold weather sets in, Archie is sitting behind the stove all ready every night when he comes home from work. - Sis . From  302-13th Ave No. Seattle, Wn.  "(illegible) (illegible)"
creator: Sis

title: Postcard with a view of Drammen, Norway (1911)
description: Suspected photograph. Color. A town by a river, with pine trees in the foreground
transcription: Transcription Recto: (NA). Transcription Verso: Oct 15th 1911 Sonday. Dear Children, The long looked for Boy arriaved in the Hagen family about 6 Oclock Saturday A.M. Mother + babe doing well. The neighbors kept their promise they are very kind to them. The Engaged Nurse is still at the other place and can not leave. A Mrs Stevens of Summerset will be with them Monday. They have had a girl for some time but she goes home every Saturday night "with a Rig of (illegible)" comes back Monday A.M. George came for Auntie last night to be with them untill this evening and Kate will stay untill nurse & girl comes. Eloise was here all night. Affect Mother
creator: Mother

title: Postcard of the Chambre des Deputes, Paris, France (1918)
description: Photograph. Black-and-white. Bridge over a body of water leading to a Romanesque building
transcription: Transcription Recto: Dear Brother. I went out and saw this tower the other day and it sure is a wonder. The Ferris wheel is near and I went on it. The tour is over 900 ft. high and the Ferris wheel is about 300 I think. Nearly all of the new arrivals in Paris get an ailment somewhat like a sore throat and so we have almost all been affected by it. I had it but am well now and keep going. The last few days and nights have been quiet. P.S I thot I was writing on another card that pictured the Eiffel Tour. We had some rain tonight so won't get an air raid.. Transcription Verso: (NA).
creator: H.D.
subject: United States; California; Los Angeles; building
